Reinforcement Learning (Q-Learning) 
▪Still assume a Markov decision process (MDP): 
▪A set of states s ∈ S 
▪A set of actions (per state) A 
▪A model T(s,a,s’) 
▪A reward function R(s,a,s’) 
▪Still looking for a policy π(s) 
 
▪New twist: don’t know T or R 
▪I.e. we don’t know which states are good or what the actions do 
▪Must actually try actions and states out to learn 
 في انـMDP
 
 بُكٌٕ عارفيٍ انـr, T
 
 عارف انـreward
 
 ٔعارف انـprobability
 
ٍنكم حانة ٔأكش 
 في انـRL
 
 يابُكَٕص عارفيٍ انـR,T 
 فانـagent
 
 عشاٌ يتعهى انـpolicy
 
 ْٕ يحتاج يتعهى قيًةT, R
 فٕٓ أكثر تعقيذا

Active Reinforcement Learning 
▪Full reinforcement learning: optimal policies (like value iteration) 
▪You don’t know the transitions T(s,a,s’) 
▪You don’t know the rewards R(s,a,s’) 
▪You choose the actions now 
▪Goal: learn the optimal policy / values 
 
▪In this case: 
▪Learner makes choices! 
▪Fundamental tradeoff: exploration vs. exploitation 
▪This is NOT offline planning! You actually take actions in the world and find 
out what happens…

Q-Learning Algorithm 
must explore in first episodes 
learning rate 
Q(s,a) ← (1 - α) Q(s,a) + α *r + 𝛾 maxa’ Q(s’,a’)+ 
 األنجٕريزو دِ دايًا بيٕصم نُتايح كٕيسة حتى نٕ يااختارش انـoptimal decision
 
 في األٔل َّأل بيختبر اختيارات يختهفة انى أٌ يصم نهـoptimal Q

Q-Learning Properties 
▪Amazing result: Q-learning converges to optimal policy -- even 
if you’re acting suboptimally! 
 
▪This is called off-policy learning 
 
▪Caveats: 
▪You have to explore enough 
▪You have to eventually make the learning rate 
 small enough

Q-Learning Properties 
▪Amazing result: Q-learning converges to optimal policy -- even 
if you’re acting suboptimally!

▪Caveats:

▪You have to eventually make the learning rate 
 small enough

▪Amazing result: Q-learning converges to optimal policy -- even 
if you’re acting suboptimally!

▪Caveats: 
▪You have to explore enough 
▪You have to eventually make the learning rate

Reinforcement Learning 
Question.1 Choose the correct answer 
 
1) …………… is anything that can be viewed as perceiving its environment through sensors and 
acting upon that environment. 
 
a) Sensor b) Agent c) Effectors d) Actuators 
 
2) …………… are the devices which affect the environment. 
 
a) Sensor b) Agent c) Effectors d) Actuators 
 
3) …………… are the components of machines that convert the energy into motion. 
 
a) Sensor b) Agent c) Effectors d) Actuators

a) s, s’ 
b) s, a

a) min b) avg c) sum d) max
